---
title: "Sim_funcoes_logistica_cutoff_paralelo"
author: "Rosemeire do Nascimento"
date: "20/03/2026"
output: pdf_document
---


```{r}
# 1. CARREGAMENTO DE PACOTES
library(tidyverse)
library(tidymodels)
library(rsample)
library(ggplot2)
library(caret)    
library(maxLik)   
library(mvtnorm)  
library(powdist)  
library(pROC)
library(PRROC)
library(future)
library(furrr)
library(progressr)


```







```{r}
future::plan(multicore, workers = 12)
handlers(global = TRUE)
```







```{r}
# 1. Configurações e variáveis globais ---------------------------------------
set.seed(1234)


# Número de features
P_FEATURES <- 20


# Parâmetros de simulação padrão (podem ser modificados pelo usuário)
NUM_SIMULATIONS <- c(1000)
CLASS_PROPORTIONS <- c(0.25, 0.10, 0.01)
N_DATA <- 10000


# Epsilon para estabilidade numérica
EPS_PROB <- 1e-12

```







```{r}
# 2. Funções utilitárias -----------------------------------------------------
# Extrai métricas de confusionMatrix (com proteção quando cm é NULL)
# utilitarias
get_metrics <- function(cm_object, prop, sim_num, method_name, cutoff_used = NA_real_, auc_roc = NA_real_, auc_pr = NA_real_) {
  if (is.null(cm_object)) {
    return(tibble(
      Proportion_Minority = prop,
      Simulation_Number = sim_num,
      Method = method_name,
      Cutoff = cutoff_used,
      Accuracy = NA_real_,
      Kappa = NA_real_,
      Sensitivity = NA_real_,
      Specificity = NA_real_,
      Pos_Pred_Value = NA_real_,
      Neg_Pred_Value = NA_real_,
      Balanced_Accuracy = NA_real_,
      F1 = NA_real_,
      AUC_ROC = NA_real_,  
      AUC_PR = NA_real_,
      Error = "model_or_cm_failed"
    ))
  }
  
  f1_val <- as.numeric(cm_object$byClass["F1"])
  if (is.nan(f1_val)) f1_val <- 0 # Trata divisão por zero se Precisão/Sensibilidade forem 0
  
  tibble(
    Proportion_Minority = prop,
    Simulation_Number = sim_num,
    Method = method_name,
    Cutoff = cutoff_used,
    Accuracy = as.numeric(cm_object$overall["Accuracy"]),
    Kappa = as.numeric(cm_object$overall["Kappa"]),
    Sensitivity = as.numeric(cm_object$byClass["Sensitivity"]),
    Specificity = as.numeric(cm_object$byClass["Specificity"]),
    Pos_Pred_Value = as.numeric(cm_object$byClass["Pos Pred Value"]),
    Neg_Pred_Value = as.numeric(cm_object$byClass["Neg Pred Value"]),
    Balanced_Accuracy = as.numeric(cm_object$byClass["Balanced Accuracy"]),
    F1 = f1_val,
    AUC_ROC = as.numeric(auc_roc),
    AUC_PR = as.numeric(auc_pr),
    Error = NA_character_
  )
}




compute_cutoff <- function(y_true_factor, probs, fallback) {
  # 1. Verificações de entrada
  probs <- as.numeric(probs)
  if (length(probs) != length(y_true_factor)) return(fallback)
  
  # 2. Garante que os níveis são 0 e 1 e lida com predições únicas
  y_true_factor <- factor(y_true_factor, levels = c("0", "1"))
  if (length(unique(probs)) == 1) return(fallback)
  
  # 3. Cálculo ROC
  roc_obj <- tryCatch(pROC::roc(y_true_factor, probs, quiet = TRUE), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(roc_obj)) return(fallback)
  
  # 4. Extração do Threshold Otimizado
  th <- tryCatch(pROC::coords(roc_obj, "best", ret = "threshold", transpose = FALSE), 
                 error = function(e) NULL)
  
  # 5. COERÇÃO ROBUSTA (Correção principal): 
  # Usa unlist para lidar com listas/tibbles complexas e pega o primeiro valor
  if (!is.null(th)) {
    th_numeric <- as.numeric(unlist(th))[1]
  } else {
    th_numeric <- NA_real_
  }
  
 # 6. Verificação de falha (Melhorado: captura NaNs e valores Infinitos)
  if (is.na(th_numeric) || is.nan(th_numeric) || is.infinite(th_numeric) || length(th_numeric) < 1) {
    return(fallback) 
  }
  
  return(th_numeric)
}

safe_confusion <- function(y_true, y_pred) {
  y_true <- as.character(y_true)
  y_pred <- as.character(y_pred)
  if (length(y_true) != length(y_pred)) return(NULL)
  y_true_f <- factor(y_true, levels = c("0","1"))
  y_pred_f <- factor(y_pred, levels = c("0","1"))
  cm <- tryCatch(caret::confusionMatrix(y_pred_f, y_true_f, positive = "1"), error = function(e) NULL)
  return(cm)
}

```







```{r}
# 3. Funções de log-verossimilhança para PL e RPL -----------------------------
logvero.PL <- function(param, y, x, EPS_PROB) {
  k <- ncol(x)
  beta <- param[1:k]
  lambda.star <- param[k+1]
  lambda <- exp(lambda.star)
  p <- powdist::pplogis(q = as.numeric(x %*% beta), mu = 0, sigma = 1, lambda = lambda)
  p[p <= EPS_PROB] <- EPS_PROB
  p[p >= 1 - EPS_PROB] <- 1 - EPS_PROB
  sum(y * log(p) + (1-y)*log(1-p))
}

logvero.RPL <- function(param, y, x, EPS_PROB) {
  k <- ncol(x)
  beta <- param[1:k]
  lambda.star <- param[k+1]
  lambda <- exp(lambda.star)
  p <- powdist::prplogis(q = as.numeric(x %*% beta), mu = 0, sigma = 1, lambda = lambda)
  p[p <= EPS_PROB] <- EPS_PROB
  p[p >= 1 - EPS_PROB] <- 1 - EPS_PROB
  sum(y * log(p) + (1-y)*log(1-p))
}

```







```{r}
# 4. Gerador de dados robusto -------------------------------------------------
gerar_df <- function(n, prop_one_minority, p_features = P_FEATURES) {
  n_1 <- round(prop_one_minority * n)
  n_0 <- n - n_1
  X0 <- mvtnorm::rmvnorm(n_0, mean = rep(0, p_features), sigma = 4 * diag(p_features))
  X1 <- mvtnorm::rmvnorm(n_1, mean = rep(1 / sqrt(p_features), p_features), sigma = diag(p_features))
  df <- as_tibble(rbind(X0, X1))
  colnames(df)[1:p_features] <- paste0("x", 1:p_features)
  df$Class <- factor(c(rep(0, n_0), rep(1, n_1)), levels = c(0,1))
  df$pesos <- ifelse(df$Class == 1, 1/prop_one_minority, 1/(1 - prop_one_minority))
  return(df)
}

```







```{r}
# 5. Funções de ajuste dos modelos (robustas) ---------------------------------
# Cada função tenta ajustar; se falhar, retorna linha NA via get_metrics


fit_model_maxLik <- function(dados, prop, sim_num, function_vero, method_name = NULL, EPS_PROB) {
  split <- rsample::initial_split(dados, prop = 0.75, strata = Class)
  train <- rsample::training(split); test <- rsample::testing(split)
  y_train <- as.numeric(as.character(train$Class))
  x_train <- as.matrix(train %>% select(starts_with("x")))
  x_test <- as.matrix(test %>% select(starts_with("x")))
  start <- rep(0, ncol(x_train)+1); start[length(start)] <- log(1)
  est <- tryCatch(maxLik(logLik = function_vero, start = start, y = y_train, x = x_train, EPS_PROB <- 1e-12,
                         method = "BFGSR", control = list(iterlim = 1000)), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(est) || any(is.na(est$estimate))) return(get_metrics(NULL, prop, sim_num, ifelse(is.null(method_name), deparse(substitute(function_vero)), method_name), cutoff_used, v_auc_roc, v_auc_pr))
  beta <- est$estimate[1:(length(est$estimate)-1)]
  lambda <- exp(est$estimate[length(est$estimate)])
  p_pred <- tryCatch({
    if (identical(function_vero, logvero.PL)) powdist::pplogis(q = as.numeric(x_test %*% beta), mu = 0, sigma = 1, lambda = lambda)
    else if (identical(function_vero, logvero.RPL)) powdist::prplogis(q = as.numeric(x_test %*% beta), mu = 0, sigma = 1, lambda = lambda)
    else 1 / (1 + exp(-(x_test %*% beta)))
  }, error = function(e) rep(NA_real_, nrow(test)))
  
  test$Class <- factor(test$Class, levels = c("0", "1"))
  
  p_pred <- as.numeric(p_pred)
  p_pred[is.na(p_pred)] <- EPS_PROB
  p_pred[p_pred <= EPS_PROB] <- EPS_PROB
  p_pred[p_pred >= 1 - EPS_PROB] <- 1 - EPS_PROB
  
   # Cálculo da AUC-ROC via pROC
  roc_obj <- tryCatch(pROC::roc(test$Class, p_pred, quiet = TRUE), error = function(e) NULL)
  v_auc_roc <- if(!is.null(roc_obj)) as.numeric(pROC::auc(roc_obj)) else NA_real_
  
  # Cálculo da AUC-PR via PRROC
  # scores.class0 recebe as probabilidades da classe positiva (1) correspondente aos uns reais
  # scores.class1 recebe as probabilidades da classe positiva (1) correspondente aos zeros reais
  pr_obj <- tryCatch({
    PRROC::pr.curve(scores.class0 = p_pred[test$Class == "1"], 
                    scores.class1 = p_pred[test$Class == "0"], 
                    curve = FALSE)
  }, error = function(e) NULL)
  v_auc_pr <- if(!is.null(pr_obj)) pr_obj$auc.integral else NA_real_
  
  cutoff <- compute_cutoff(test$Class, p_pred, fallback = prop)
  if (length(p_pred) != nrow(test)) return(get_metrics(NULL, prop, sim_num, ifelse(is.null(method_name), deparse(substitute(function_vero)), method_name), cutoff, v_auc_roc, v_auc_pr))
  pred <- ifelse(p_pred >= cutoff, "1", "0")
  cm <- safe_confusion(test$Class, pred)
  return(get_metrics(cm, prop, sim_num, ifelse(is.null(method_name), deparse(substitute(function_vero)), method_name), cutoff, v_auc_roc, v_auc_pr))
}

fit_model_glm_weighted <- function(dados, prop, sim_num, EPS_PROB) {
  split <- rsample::initial_split(dados, prop = 0.75, strata = Class)
  train <- rsample::training(split); test <- rsample::testing(split)
  est_glm <- tryCatch(suppressWarnings(glm(Class ~ ., data = train %>% select(-pesos), family = binomial(link = "logit"), weights = train$pesos)), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(est_glm)) return(get_metrics(NULL, prop, sim_num, "GLM_Weighted"))
  p_pred <- tryCatch(as.numeric(predict(est_glm, newdata = test %>% select(-Class, -pesos), type = "response")), error = function(e) rep(NA_real_, nrow(test)))
  p_pred[is.na(p_pred)] <- EPS_PROB
  p_pred[p_pred <= EPS_PROB] <- EPS_PROB
  p_pred[p_pred >= 1 - EPS_PROB] <- 1 - EPS_PROB
  
  roc_obj <- tryCatch(pROC::roc(test$Class, p_pred, quiet = TRUE), error = function(e) NULL)
  v_auc_roc <- if(!is.null(roc_obj)) as.numeric(pROC::auc(roc_obj)) else NA_real_
  
  pr_obj <- tryCatch({
    PRROC::pr.curve(scores.class0 = p_pred[test$Class == "1"], 
                    scores.class1 = p_pred[test$Class == "0"], 
                    curve = FALSE)
  }, error = function(e) NULL)
  v_auc_pr <- if(!is.null(pr_obj)) pr_obj$auc.integral else NA_real_
  
  cutoff <- compute_cutoff(test$Class, p_pred, fallback = prop)
  pred <- ifelse(p_pred >= cutoff, "1", "0")
  cm <- safe_confusion(test$Class, pred)
  return(get_metrics(cm, prop, sim_num, "GLM_Weighted", cutoff, v_auc_roc, v_auc_pr))
}

fit_model_glm_standard <- function(dados, prop, sim_num, EPS_PROB) {
  split <- rsample::initial_split(dados, prop = 0.75, strata = Class)
  train <- rsample::training(split); test <- rsample::testing(split)
  est_glm <- tryCatch(suppressWarnings(glm(Class ~ ., data = train %>% select(-pesos), family = binomial(link = "logit"))), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(est_glm)) return(get_metrics(NULL, prop, sim_num, "GLM_Standard"))
  p_pred <- tryCatch(as.numeric(predict(est_glm, newdata = test %>% select(-Class, -pesos), type = "response")), error = function(e) rep(NA_real_, nrow(test)))
  p_pred[is.na(p_pred)] <- EPS_PROB
  p_pred[p_pred <= EPS_PROB] <- EPS_PROB
  p_pred[p_pred >= 1 - EPS_PROB] <- 1 - EPS_PROB
  
  roc_obj <- tryCatch(pROC::roc(test$Class, p_pred, quiet = TRUE), error = function(e) NULL)
  v_auc_roc <- if(!is.null(roc_obj)) as.numeric(pROC::auc(roc_obj)) else NA_real_
  
  pr_obj <- tryCatch({
    PRROC::pr.curve(scores.class0 = p_pred[test$Class == "1"], 
                    scores.class1 = p_pred[test$Class == "0"], 
                    curve = FALSE)
  }, error = function(e) NULL)
  v_auc_pr <- if(!is.null(pr_obj)) pr_obj$auc.integral else NA_real_
  
  
  
  cutoff <- compute_cutoff(test$Class, p_pred, fallback = prop)
  pred <- ifelse(p_pred >= cutoff, "1", "0")
  cm <- safe_confusion(test$Class, pred)
  return(get_metrics(cm, prop, sim_num, "GLM_Standard", cutoff, v_auc_roc, v_auc_pr))
}

```







```{r}
# 6. Runner e função principal de simulação ----------------------------------
CLASS_PROPORTIONS <- c(0.25, 0.10, 0.01)
NUM_SIMULATIONS <- c(1000) # testar com 10 para começar
results <- tibble()
with_progress({
  for (prop in CLASS_PROPORTIONS) {
    for (sim_total in NUM_SIMULATIONS) {
      
      message(sprintf("\n>>> Executando: Prop = %.2f | Total de Sims = %d", prop, sim_total))
      
      # O local() resolve o erro de "finite life span"
      out <- local({
        # Agora o progressor está protegido dentro deste ambiente local
        p <- progressor(steps = sim_total)
        
        future_map_dfr(1:sim_total, ~{
          p() # Atualiza a barra
          
          dados <- gerar_df(n = N_DATA, prop_one_minority = prop)
      
          bind_rows(
            fit_model_maxLik(dados, prop, .x, logvero.PL, method_name = "GLM_PL"),
            fit_model_maxLik(dados, prop, .x, logvero.RPL, method_name = "GLM_RPL"),
            fit_model_glm_weighted(dados, prop, .x),
            fit_model_glm_standard(dados, prop, .x)
          )
        }, .options = furrr_options(seed = TRUE))
      })
      
      results <- bind_rows(results, out)
      
      # Dica: Salvar parcial após cada combinação de Prop e Sim
      # readr::write_csv(results, "results_parcial.csv")
    }
  }
})

results <- results %>% mutate(Method = as.character(Method))
results

readr::write_csv(results, "results.csv")

```







```{r}
# 7. Agregação e visualização -----------------------------------------------
metric_tab <- results

metric_summary <- metric_tab %>%
  group_by(Proportion_Minority, Method) %>%
  summarise(
    Acuracia = mean(Accuracy, na.rm = TRUE),
    Acuracia_Balanceada = mean(Balanced_Accuracy, na.rm = TRUE),
    Sensibilidade = mean(Sensitivity, na.rm = TRUE),
    Especificidade = mean(Specificity, na.rm = TRUE),
    AUC_ROC = mean(AUC_ROC, na.rm = TRUE),  
    AUC_PR = mean(AUC_PR, na.rm = TRUE),
    Cutoff_Medio = mean(Cutoff, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

metric_summary

```







```{r}

# 8. Graficos ------------------------------------------------

# metric_long <- metric_summary %>%
#   pivot_longer(cols = c(Acuracia, Acuracia_Balanceada, Sensibilidade, Especificidade, AUC_ROC, AUC_PR),
#                names_to = "Metrica", values_to = "Valor") %>%
#   mutate(Prop_Label = factor(Proportion_Minority, levels = c(0.25,0.10,0.01),
#                              labels = c("P = 25%","P = 10%","P = 1%")),
#          Metrica = factor(Metrica, levels = c("Acuracia","Acuracia_Balanceada","Sensibilidade","Especificidade", "AUC_ROC", "AUC_PR")))
# 
# ggplot(metric_long, aes(x = Method, y = Valor, fill = Method)) +
#   geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
#   geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(is.na(Valor), "NA", scales::percent(Valor, accuracy = 0.1))),
#             vjust = -0.3, size = 3) +
#   facet_grid(Metrica ~ Prop_Label) +
#   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,1), labels = scales::percent) +
#   labs(title = "Comparação Média das Métricas por Proporção de Minoria",
#        x = "Método", y = "Valor") +
#   theme_minimal() + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
# 
# ggsave("comparacao_metricas.png", width = 11, height = 10, dpi = 300)
# 
# 
# # 1. Transformar para formato longo incluindo todas as métricas calculadas
# metric_long <- metric_summary %>%
#   pivot_longer(cols = c(Acuracia, Acuracia_Balanceada, Sensibilidade, Especificidade, AUC_ROC, AUC_PR),
#                names_to = "Metrica", values_to = "Valor") %>%
#   mutate(Prop_Label = factor(Proportion_Minority, levels = c(0.25,0.10,0.01),
#                              labels = c("P = 25%","P = 10%","P = 1%")),
#          Metrica = factor(Metrica, levels = c("Acuracia","Acuracia_Balanceada","Sensibilidade","Especificidade", "AUC_ROC", "AUC_PR")))
# 
# # 2. Gerar o painel visual (Corrigido para evitar sobreposição)
# ggplot(metric_long, aes(x = Method, y = Valor, fill = Method)) +
#   geom_col(show.legend = FALSE, alpha = 0.8) +
#   
#   # 1. Rótulos: arredondamos e usamos 'vjust' para dar um respiro acima da barra
#   geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(is.na(Valor), "NA", format(round(Valor, 4), nsmall = 4))),
#             vjust = -0.5,  # Sobe um pouco mais o texto
#             size = 2.5) +    # Ajuste o tamanho conforme necessário
#   
#   facet_grid(Metrica ~ Prop_Label) + 
#   
#   # 2. Expansão do eixo Y: O 'expand' garante que o topo não corte o texto
#   scale_y_continuous(
#     limits = c(0, 1.1),
#     labels = function(x) format(round(x, 4), nsmall = 4),
#     expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15)) # Adiciona 15% de espaço no topo
#   ) +
#   
#   labs(
#     title = "Comparativo de Modelos por Proporção de Minoria",
#     x = "Método", 
#     y = "Valor Nominal"
#   ) +
#   theme_minimal() +
#   theme(
#     axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
#     strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10), 
#     panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines")
#   )
# 
# ggsave(
#   filename = "comparativo_modelos_por_proporcao.png",
#   plot = last_plot(), # Garante que está pegando o último gráfico gerado
#   width = 11,         # Largura em polegadas
#   height = 10,         # Altura em polegadas
#   dpi = 300,          # Alta resolução para impressão ou relatórios
#   bg = "white"        # Define fundo branco (evita fundos transparentes em alguns sistemas)
# )

# 8. Gráficos (Código unificado e otimizado) ----------------------------------
metric_long <- metric_summary %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(Acuracia, Acuracia_Balanceada, Sensibilidade, Especificidade, AUC_ROC, AUC_PR),
               names_to = "Metrica", values_to = "Valor") %>%
  mutate(Prop_Label = factor(Proportion_Minority, levels = c(0.25, 0.10, 0.01),
                             labels = c("P = 25%","P = 10%","P = 1%")),
         Metrica = factor(Metrica, levels = c("Acuracia","Acuracia_Balanceada","Sensibilidade","Especificidade", "AUC_ROC", "AUC_PR")))

# Gerar o painel visual final corrigido
ggplot(metric_long, aes(x = Method, y = Valor, fill = Method)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(is.na(Valor), "NA", format(round(Valor, 4), nsmall = 4))),
            vjust = -0.5,  
            size = 2.5) +    
  facet_grid(Metrica ~ Prop_Label) + 
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = function(x) format(round(x, 4), nsmall = 4),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15)) 
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Comparativo de Modelos por Proporção de Minoria",
    x = "Método", 
    y = "Valor Nominal"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10), 
    panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines")
  )

ggsave(
  filename = "comparativo_modelos_por_proporcao.png",
  plot = last_plot(), 
  width = 11,         
  height = 10,         
  dpi = 300,          
  bg = "white"        
)


```







```{r}
# 9. Salvamento de resultados ------------------------------------------------
readr::write_csv(metric_tab, "metric_tab_results.csv")
message("Salvo em metric_tab_results.csv")

```
